mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate discount rates and borrowing costs, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations, causing institutional investors to liquidate equities and producing negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns exceed 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or t-statistic for return difference falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no significant difference in forward returns vs baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals acute supply chain disruption, triggering short-term risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power during stable supply regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with stable or increasing federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses equity multiples, producing negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Stagflationary periods yield positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or joint signal IC remains below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDP growth levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230, elevated inflation expectations prompt aggressive equity positioning to hedge purchasing power loss, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in < 40% of triggered instances, or signal reverses direction when real rates turn positive.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust labor market expansion, supporting earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power during labor market cooling phases.
